name: Marshall Allen
description: American jazz saxophonist, flutist and oboist (born 1924)
abstract: Marshall Belford Allen (born May 25, 1924) is an American free jazz and avant-garde jazz alto saxophone player. He also performs on flute, oboe, piccolo, and the EWI.

interviews.title: Marshall Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 19, 2021

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Marshall back then. You know? And Ronnie Boykins, John Gilmore.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: I’m not sure when James Spaulding was in the band but he’s still with us. So ain’t but a handful of people, I would say maybe four or five at the most, that go back as deep as Marshall and Julian Priester and myself played in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with Sun Ra and his Arkestra... At the time the band was John Gilmore, bass player Ronnie Boykins, Marshall Allen...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: He brought all his members from Chicago, mainly Ronnie Boykins... Marshall Allen, alto... I got there in 1960.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 17, 2022 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: Playing in that band, well let’s see, that’s where I met John Gilmore as well... so Marshall Allen, I was checking out Marshall and Sunny and a bunch of the other cats.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022
